benchmark_details.name: HaluEval-Wild
benchmark_details.overview: HaluEval-Wild is the first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate LLM hallucinations in the wild using a dataset of 500 challenging user queries categorized into five distinct types, facilitating a fine-grained analysis of hallucination rates across various LLMs.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: The primary objective of HaluEval-Wild is to provide a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate and enhance the robustness of language models in the face of real-world hallucination challenges.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: While HaluEval-Wild offers valuable insights into LLM hallucinations, it may not fully encapsulate the breadth of everyday user-LLM interactions and relies on manual verification for categorization accuracy.

data.source: Challenging queries curated from the ShareGPT dataset, filtered for complexity and hallucination potential.
data.annotation: Manual verification of query categorization and reference answer generation.

methodology.calculation: Models are evaluated based on the proportion of hallucinated responses relative to the reference answers.
methodology.interpretation: A response is considered non-hallucinatory if it matches the reference answer or admits the inability to fulfill the request.
methodology.baseline_results: Hallucination rates range significantly across models, with identifiable trends related to model types and architectures.
methodology.validation: Validation included manual verification and a rigorous filtering process against hallucination-prone patterns.

ethical_and_legal_considerations.privacy_and_anonymity: Data entries were reviewed to ensure no personally identifiable information (PII) is included.
